required_inputs: request scope and intended BPO action
specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
tenant approval, SOW, supplier-risk, financial, privacy, and quality-release authority rules
refusal_triggers: request asks for spend approval, contract amendment, supplier substitution, supplier-risk approval, legal interpretation, or client-facing final signoff
request treats BPO queue or availability tracking as authority to override procurement, quality, privacy, security, HR, payroll, or regulated-process controls
request collapses the administrative-support and business-services context into a generic outsourcing checklist without service-family records

required_inputs: authoritative BPO, outsourcing, service-contracting, supplier-risk, records, privacy, and service-organization source set
source freshness and decay policy
tenant SOW, policy, contract, and approval precedence rules
refusal_triggers: required public source class or tenant source context is missing
public baseline, tenant policy, client SOW, and vendor records materially conflict
source or tenant policy is stale enough to change procurement, availability, privacy, records, or service posture

required_inputs: client master, contract/SOW, service catalog, work orders, queue extracts, runbooks, and SLA records
vendor, procurement, PO, inventory, asset, license, and approved-provider records
records repository, retention schedule, chain-of-custody logs, access records, GRC issues, and audit evidence
refusal_triggers: critical BPO service artifacts are missing, stale, contradictory, or untraceable
queue, vendor, procurement, inventory, license, IAM, DMS, or SLA systems disagree materially
records cannot be linked to an accountable owner or authoritative system of record

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory and retrieval hooks
tenant owner and approval matrix
service-family, queue, vendor, inventory, license, access, and SLA dependency state
refusal_triggers: critical ticketing, BPM, CLM, vendor, procurement, inventory, license, IAM, DMS, GRC, or SLA system is unavailable
required owner or approver is unresolved
record location, retrieval hook, queue truth source, or approval threshold is missing

required_inputs: confidence-impacting missing service facts
policy, SOW, source, or vendor-record conflict indicators
system, approval, or authority uncertainty flags
refusal_triggers: service family, jurisdiction, SOW, queue truth source, approved-vendor status, inventory/license state, privacy classification, or approval map is missing
source precedence conflict is unresolved
evidence sufficiency or owner authority cannot be established

required_inputs: scope outcome
handoff context and evidence packet
adjacent-lane owner map
refusal_triggers: request belongs to procurement approval, supplier risk, contract management, finance, legal, privacy, security incident, HR/payroll, quality release, or regulated-process ownership
request requires formal client-facing, executive, legal, quality, or financial signoff
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified from the service-family or work-item context

required_inputs: queue demand, backlog, SLA, and capacity records
vendor capacity, seat, license, access, asset, inventory, and operating-input status
client SOW, service catalog, runbook, and service-prerequisite records
required_outputs: administrative service status summary with queue state, blocker, and next owner
refusal_triggers: availability cannot be established from authoritative queue, vendor, inventory, license, or access records
service prerequisites block safe action
reported supply, capacity, or operating-input state cannot be tied to current system-of-record evidence

required_inputs: purchase request, PO, approved-vendor, supplier-risk, and contract/SOW records
vendor commitments, service-capacity constraints, rate card, and replenishment request
approval thresholds, substitution rules, and financial-control boundaries
required_outputs: procurement or replenishment recommendation with constraints and dependencies
refusal_triggers: vendor action requires spend approval, contract modification, approved-supplier override, or supplier-risk approval
requested substitution, capacity change, or service provider change exceeds documented lane authority
procurement evidence or approval prerequisites are incomplete

required_inputs: shortage, hold, backlog, SLA-risk, and vendor-risk posture
inventory, license, operating-input, access, and provider capacity evidence
approval, privacy, quality, supplier-risk, or records blockers
refusal_triggers: critical shortage, queue hold, access hold, privacy hold, supplier hold, or quality hold remains unresolved
shortage workaround requires supplier substitution, spend approval, client approval, quality release, or regulated authority
SLA risk cannot be attributed to authoritative queue, vendor, or capacity evidence

required_inputs: BPO service-family context
client service artifacts and queue records
vendor, procurement, capacity, inventory, SLA, records, access, and service-organization evidence
refusal_triggers: industry-specific BPO artifacts are missing
request treats outsourcing or administrative support as a cosmetic label
service family, jurisdiction, client model, or regulated subprocess is omitted in a way that changes artifact expectations
